classObjective: Model real-world situations with linear equations in slope-intercept form and determine whether a point lies on a given line.
workedExamples: 5-1, Example 3 — Write an Equation Given Two Points (continued)
5-1, Example 4 — Write Equations from Data and Models and Check Point Membership
warmUp: Does the point (4, 11) lie on the line y = 2x + 3?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: population, pricing, and temperature contexts; checking point-on-line membership by substitution
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice modeling real-world situations with linear equations and checking point membership
independentPractice: Practice problems modeling real-world situations and determining whether points lie on given lines
assessment: Exit ticket: write a linear model for the population of a town growing by 250 people per year, starting at 5000, and check if year 8 population equals 6750
capReflection: How does checking whether a point lies on a line help you validate a real-world model?
